title: Muscle rub balm
subtitle: A cooling anhydrous balm with peppermint and eucalyptus essential oils for tired muscles
excerpt: Peppermint and eucalyptus essential oils produce a pronounced cooling and warming sensation on the skin, making them the central actives in this muscle rub. The beeswax-based balm applies smoothly and allows the oils to absorb while providing a small amount of warming friction during application.
sourceNotes: Peppermint and eucalyptus essential oil usage rates for topical muscle products from IFRA and aromatherapy safety references.

recipe.yieldDescription: 60 g muscle rub balm (fills a 60 ml tin)
recipe.temperatureNote: Melt wax and oils gently. Add essential oils once below 40 degrees Celsius.
recipe.shelfLifeNotes: 12 months. Discard if the balm smells rancid or the essential oil scent fades entirely. Do not use on children under 2 or during the first trimester of pregnancy.

recipeTools.notes: Accurate to 0.1 g for essential oils

glossaryTerms.definition: Peppermint essential oil contains menthol, which activates cold-sensitive TRPM8 receptors in the skin, producing a sensation of cooling without actually lowering skin temperature. This is the basis of most topical cooling products.
The primary active compound in eucalyptus essential oil. It has mild analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties and contributes to the penetrating warmth felt after the initial cooling of peppermint.
Containing no water. Anhydrous products require no preservative and are stable at room temperature.

The percentage of essential oil in a finished product. For a rinse-off or massage product applied to non-sensitive areas of the body, up to 5% is generally within safe limits. Peppermint and eucalyptus at 2% each sit well within this range.

items.prepNote: 1% of formula; optional; adds additional warming depth

content.text: Melt the beeswax, coconut oil, and olive oil together in a double boiler until the wax is fully liquid. Remove from heat. Allow to cool to below 40 degrees Celsius. Add the peppermint essential oil, eucalyptus essential oil, and camphor if using. Stir well. Pour into the metal tin before the mixture sets. Leave for 1 hour at room temperature.

Store in the tin at room temperature. Shelf life is 12 months. The cooling sensation may feel stronger in cooler temperatures. Do not apply to broken skin, eyes, or mucous membranes. Do not use on children under 2 or near the face of any child. Avoid use during the first trimester of pregnancy.
